event.name: OpenAI Build Week Packet
event.deadline: July 21, 2026, 5:00 PM PT, per Devpost overview copy.
required_materials: Devpost says builders should create a working project using Codex with GPT-5.6 and can enter one of four tracks: Apps for Your Life, Work and Productivity, Developer Tools, or Education

Required submission materials now include a chosen category, project description, public YouTube demo video under three minutes with voiceover covering how Codex and GPT-5.6 were used, repository URL with README/setup/sample data, and a `/feedback` Codex Session ID from the primary build thread
The theme fits this workspace and the user's Codex-heavy workflow

OpenAI still needs a `/feedback` Session ID and live GPT-5.6 proof before Devpost final submit; the public repository, public YouTube demo URL, Devpost event registration, proof-boundary gate, and public URL verification gate are now verified locally
OpenAI/Codex credits are available only through the event resource flow and must remain prepaid/no-auto-top-up before any live GPT-5.6 evidence run

Public YouTube demo video under three minutes with voiceover that covers what was built, how Codex was used, and how GPT-5.6 was used
Code repository, public with relevant license or private and shared with `testing@devpost.com` and `build-week-event@openai.com`
account_requirements: OpenAI's Build Week page says the challenge opens July 13, 2026, submissions are due July 21, 2026, judging runs July 22 to August 7, and winners are announced August 12
Devpost now shows OpenAI Build Week running online through July 21, 2026, 5:00 PM PDT, with $100,000 in cash prizes and 22,514 participants on public surfaces during this recheck

source_notes: Devpost now shows OpenAI Build Week running online through July 21, 2026, 5:00 PM PDT, with $100,000 in cash prizes and 22,514 participants on public surfaces during this recheck
Devpost Official Rules are now posted; public rule/search surfaces list the submission period as July 13, 2026, 9:00 AM Pacific Time through July 21, 2026, 5:00 PM Pacific Time
Public deadline: July 21, 2026, 5:00 PM PT, per Devpost overview copy

Rules state: Official Rules are now posted; still do not accept final terms or submit if the logged-in flow asks for payment, travel, tax, banking, government ID, employment/IP-transfer commitments beyond ordinary hackathon rules, CAPTCHA bypass, or unsupported eligibility facts
Official rules and FAQs are now available, so implementation can proceed without the earlier rules-not-posted stop
Qwen Cloud has a nearly complete local/public packet and a July 20, 2026 PDT deadline

Builders lose hackathons at the last mile: rules drift, proof links are missing, demo videos overrun, and teams make unsupported claims under deadline pressure. SubmitOps Scout uses Codex to turn a repo plus competition URLs into a ranked submission command center: source snapshots, eligibility gates, judging-rubric fit, proof ledger, Devpost drafts, demo scripts, blocker lists, and validation commands
It has a real, demonstrated audience: builders shipping under deadline
2. Store normalized opportunity facts: deadline, eligibility, required accounts, required materials, judging criteria, proof links, and blockers
2. Show source facts, deadline conversion, required materials, and excluded/unknown eligibility facts

draft_answers.title: SubmitOps Scout: Codex-Powered Submission Command Center
draft_answers.short_description: SubmitOps Scout helps builders turn a project repository and hackathon rules into a ready-to-submit packet: eligibility gates, proof links, Devpost drafts, demo scripts, validation commands, and blockers.
draft_answers.what_it_does: It reads public competition pages, scans local repo evidence, maps judging criteria to proof, drafts submission copy, and refuses unsupported claims until a human supplies missing account, eligibility, legal, payment, or publication facts.
draft_answers.why_it_can_win: It is a practical Codex-native developer tool with a live deadline use case. Instead of only generating code, it helps teams ship truthful, judge-ready submissions under pressure.

checks.name: official rules captured
checks.detail: Packet records Official Rules source state.
